posture: Watch — one fresh, dated logistics signal cleared the NSW-REZ relevance bar in the 24 hours to Sunday morning: on the Central-West Orana Port-to-REZ route, oversize-overmass (OSOM) deliveries to Squadron Energy's Uungula Wind Farm are set to recommence from Monday 13 July 2026, weather and conditions permitting, following completion of the Goolma Road / Mitchell Highway intersection upgrade — the first concrete recommencement date to firm up the 'deliveries begin around July 2026' thread the radar has carried as background. This is a weekend, so news flow was light and all heavier threads are prior-digest carry-forwards with no fresh movement: EnergyCo's Draft New England REZ Access Scheme Declaration remains on public exhibition (released 7 July, feedback by 6 August, targeted end-September ministerial declaration); the New England corridor field-investigation program runs July-September across the refined 1km Muswellbrook-Walcha study corridor to Bayswater (~150 affected landholders) amid live Walcha High Country Guardians opposition, the Coalition's late-June policy to scrap the 500kV corridor and cap the zone (redirecting to proposed City and Outback REZs) into the March 2027 election run-up, and a community-group alliance's threat to disengage from consultation; and on Central-West Orana, ACEREZ's late-June position that it has recommenced access-track work under stronger controls remains the latest word while the DPHI rock-shelter heritage assessment stays open.
summary: A quiet weekend scan surfaced a single fresh NSW-REZ-relevant development: OSOM (oversize-overmass) turbine-component deliveries to Squadron Energy's Uungula Wind Farm, moving via the Port-of-Newcastle-to-Central-West-Orana route, are scheduled to recommence from Monday 13 July 2026 (weather and conditions permitting) after a pause for completion of the Goolma Road / Mitchell Highway intersection upgrade. This is the first firm recommencement date on a Port-to-REZ logistics thread the radar has carried only as generic background, and is logged as situational-awareness 'inform'. All other threads — the New England Access Scheme consultation window, the July-September field-investigation program, the Walcha-area and Coalition opposition and the alliance's disengagement threat, and the ACEREZ Central-West Orana rock-shelter matter — are prior-digest entries with no fresh movement in the window and are deduplicated out per the writing rules.

items.headline: Oversized turbine-component deliveries to Uungula via the Port-to-REZ route set to restart Monday
items.summary: Oversize-overmass (OSOM) deliveries carrying turbine components to Squadron Energy's Uungula Wind Farm in the Central-West Orana REZ are scheduled to recommence from Monday 13 July 2026, weather and conditions permitting, once the upgrade of the Goolma Road / Mitchell Highway intersection is complete. The loads travel from the Port of Newcastle via the Golden Highway, Saxa Road, the Mitchell Highway/Goolma Road intersection and Twelve Mile Road to the project site, and are the first use of the Port-to-REZ corridor for oversized renewable equipment, with movements expected to run for 10-12 months.
items.so_what: The restart puts a firm date on the first sustained oversized-load campaign on the Central-West Orana Port-to-REZ route, concentrating traffic, road-condition and community-disruption exposure along a defined regional corridor; smooth execution supports the schedule for connected generation while any weather delays, road incidents or local friction over the intersection and haul route are the early markers EnergyCo should track for delivery-risk and social-licence spillover.
